text: The surgeon performs lateral to medial mobilization of the sigmoid and descending colon, continuing cephalad dissection to the splenic flexure. The inferior mesenteric artery is ligated using a vessel sealer, with ligation extending to the level of the rectum. The colon's mobilization is confirmed before proceeding with the rectum transection.

The surgeon uses a grasper to secure the proximal colon through the twelve millimeter trocar site. A Proline suture is applied to mark the rectum for future colostomy reversal. The proximal colon is exteriorized through the trocar site, and the ostomy is matured.
